headline: Jury: Johnson & Johnson Liabile in Case Linking Baby Powder to Cancer
summary: On Friday, a Pennsylvania state-court jury in Philadelphia ordered Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) to pay $250,000 to the family of Gayle Emerson, finding the company liable in a case blaming its talc baby powder for her ovarian cancer.

headline: Shutdown Leaves TSA Staff Unpaid, Travel Delays Loom
summary: On Saturday, the Department of Homeland Security entered a funding lapse that keeps Transportation Security Administration officers on the job at U.S. airports without pay, as lawmakers remain deadlocked and a Kalshi betting contract tied to a shutdown drew more than $6 million in wagers.

headline: Nvidia's Huang To Skip India AI Impact Summit
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has canceled his appearance at India's Feb. 16–20 AI Impact Summit for unspecified reasons, though other top tech leaders are still expected to attend.

headline: Berkshire's Abel Lauds Kraft Heinz Turnaround, Eyes Future Split
summary: On Saturday, The Kraft Heinz Company drew fresh attention after Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel backed CEO Steve Cahillane for halting work on a previously announced plan to split Kraft from Heinz.

headline: Rubio Urges Europe To Join Trump Security Strategy
summary: Marco Rubio told Europe at the Munich conference to back a Trump-aligned, conditional alliance focused on border control and reshaping global institutions.

headline: Grant Cardone Is Selling A Mega Mansion For Bitcoin Only And Says He'll Be 'Better Off' By 2029 — But Will Anyone Bite?
summary: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) may have been hammered in the ongoing slump, but is it enough to swap your digital fortune for a beachfront luxury pad?

headline: Amazon Intern Tripled Her Big Tech Paycheck And Built A $302,000 Side Hustle That Outpays Her Day Job
summary: Sundas Khalid tripled her Big Tech pay, became a principal at Google, and earned $302K through her content business, providing leverage in a volatile job market.

headline: EXCLUSIVE: 'Inference Infrastructure Gap To Widen' — Bitcoin Miner Cango's AI Pivot Signals An ETF Opportunity
summary: Crypto miner Cango is pivoting to AI infrastructure. A senior Cango executive cites growing power demand as a reason. This shift hides ETF opportunities.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Cloudfare, Meta, Lyft — And AI Fears Spook Investors
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: GameStop, Palantir, Tesla And More: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Buzzing About This Week
summary: Retail investors talked up five hot stocks this week (Feb. 9 to Feb. 13) on X and Reddit's r/WallStreetBets, driven by retail hype, earnings, AI buzz, and corporate news flow.

headline: CORRECTION: Mohawk Industries Q4 Adj. EPS $2.00 Beats $1.98 Estimate, Sales $2.699B Beat $2.682B Estimate
OpenAI's Engineering Lead Says AI Era Will Create Thousands Of Niche Startups: 'It's Hard For Me To Imagine...'
summary: Sherwin Wu says AI will likely drive a surge in smaller startups, reshaping the startup and venture capital landscape.

headline: Trump Administration Moves Venezuela Oil Revenue From Qatar To US Treasury As Sales Top $1 Billion
summary: The Donald Trump administration has shifted Venezuelan oil revenue from a U.S.-controlled account in Qatar to the United States Department of the Treasury after sales topped $1 billion, following the detention of Nicolás Maduro.

headline: Trump Could Order Potential Weeks-Long Military Operations Against Iran: Report
summary: President Donald Trump could authorize weeks-long military operations against Iran as the Pentagon boosts forces in the Middle East, even as nuclear talks continue in Oman and new diplomatic meetings are set in Geneva.

headline: Trump Administration Promises Gateway Tunnel Funding After Missing Court-Ordered Deadline
summary: Federal funding for the $16 billion Gateway rail tunnel will resume after a four-month suspension, though construction remains paused until the $205 million in overdue payments arrives.

headline: Dan Ives Calls AI-Driven Software Selloff 'Most Disconnected Trade,' Says Salesforce And ServiceNow Are Historic Buys
summary: Dan Ives said Wall Street is overestimating AI's near-term threat to enterprise software, calling the selloff in Salesforce and ServiceNow a major buying opportunity despite recent stock declines.

headline: DOJ Sues Harvard For Withholding Admissions Data, Alleges Discrimination In Latest Escalation
summary: The DOJ has filed a new lawsuit against Harvard, alleging the university withheld admissions data needed to assess potential discriminatory practices following a 2023 Supreme Court ruling.

headline: Trump Threatens Executive Order Mandating Voter ID And Proof Of Citizenship If Senate Blocks SAVE Act: 'Issue That Must Be Fought'
summary: President Donald Trump said he may issue an executive order mandating voter ID and proof of citizenship if the Senate blocks the House-passed SAVE Act, as the bill faces uncertain prospects amid opposition from key lawmakers and criticism from Barack Obama, while Elon Musk has urged stricter voter registration rules.

headline: DOGE Open-Sources Largest Medicaid Dataset In Agency History As Elon Musk Touts Transparency Move: 'It's A State Of Mind'
summary: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has open-sourced what it calls the largest Medicaid dataset in its history, covering provider-level claims from 2018 through 2024 and designed to help detect fraud.

headline: Taylor Swift Forces Bedding Company To Drop 'Swift Home' Trademark : Report
summary: Cathay Home Inc. withdrew its "Swift Home" trademark application after Taylor Swift's company, TAS Rights Management, opposed it at the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office, arguing the logo resembled her signature and could mislead consumers into believing she endorsed the bedding products.

headline: Pentagon Adds Alibaba, BYD To Chinese Military List, Then Reportedly Pulls Filing As Trump-Xi Meet Looms
summary: The Pentagon briefly added Alibaba and BYD to its list of companies with alleged ties to the Chinese military, a move that could heighten U.S.-China tensions ahead of the April summit between Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping.

headline: US Tax Refunds Trending Higher Early This Season, Early IRS Data Shows
summary: Americans are on track for larger tax refunds this year, early IRS data suggests, but filing activity and processing remain lighter than last year.

headline: Trump To Reportedly Push Iran And Russia-Ukraine Talks In Geneva As Israel Monitors Potential Deals Tied To Regional Security
summary: The U.S. is set to hold separate Geneva talks with Iran over its nuclear program and with Russia and Ukraine over ending the nearly four-year war, as President Donald Trump pushes for progress under looming deadlines and Israel watches closely over security concerns.

Trading Halt: Halted at 7:50:00 p.m. ET - Trading Halt: Halt News Pending
